questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shrinkage limit is defined as the water content at which:
options: No further volume reduction occurs on drying
questions.solution: Shrinkage limit = water content below which no further volume change on drying.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The vertical deflection limit for beams and slabs (span/effective depth) for spans up to 10 m as per IS 456 is:
questions.solution: Basic L/d: SS = 20, cantilever = 7, continuous = 26 (IS 456 clause 23.2.1).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The purpose of aeration in water treatment is:
options: Removal of taste, odour, iron, and manganese
questions.solution: Aeration = gas exchange: removes volatile organics, adds O₂, oxidizes Fe/Mn.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The workability of concrete for RCC work as per IS 456 by slump test should be:
questions.solution: Slump for RCC = 25-75 mm (depending on placement conditions).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 5 m clay layer (Cc = 0.3, e₀ = 0.8) under an initial effective stress of 80 kPa receives a 60 kPa increment. The primary consolidation settlement is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Primary consolidation of a normally consolidated clay follows the logarithmic compression law.
**Formula.** $S_c=\tfrac{C_c H}{1+e_0}\log_{10}\tfrac{\sigma_0+\Delta\sigma}{\sigma_0}$
**Given.** C_c = 0.3, H = 5 m, e₀ = 0.8, σ₀ = 80 kPa, Δσ = 60 kPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.3\times5000}{1+0.8}\log_{10}\tfrac{80+60}{80}$ mm.
**Answer.** $S_c\approx 202.5$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an over-reinforced beam, failure occurs by:
options: Crushing of concrete before steel yields
questions.solution: Over-reinforced: concrete crushes first (brittle failure). Under-reinforced: steel yields first (ductile).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) measures:
options: Oxygen required by microorganisms to decompose organic matter
questions.solution: BOD = O₂ consumed by aerobic microorganisms during 5-day incubation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The initial setting time of Ordinary Portland Cement as per IS is:
questions.solution: Initial setting time ≥ 30 min; final setting time ≤ 600 min for OPC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A strip footing of width 1.5 m is founded at depth 2 m. Soil: c=15 kPa, γ=18 kN/m³, Nc=25.1, Nq=12.7, Nγ=9.7. The ultimate bearing capacity (Terzaghi) is:
questions.solution: $q_u = cN_c + \gamma D_f N_q + 0.5\gamma B N_\gamma = 964.7$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The development length Ld of a bar is given by:
questions.solution: Ld = φσs/(4τbd), where φ is bar diameter and τbd is design bond stress.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The flocculation process is aided by:
questions.solution: Flocculation = gentle agitation to promote particle agglomeration.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slump test on fresh concrete measures its:
questions.solution: The slump test is a measure of the workability (consistency) of fresh concrete.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The active earth pressure coefficient Ka is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M30 concrete, width 230 mm, effective depth 450 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 30 MPa, b = 230 mm, d = 450 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times30\times230\times450^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 192.8$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The average domestic per-capita water demand in Indian cities is taken as about:
questions.solution: A domestic demand of about 135 lpcd is commonly assumed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The water permeability of concrete depends mainly on:
questions.solution: Lower w/c + proper curing = less capillary pores = lower permeability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A retaining wall of height 8 m retains soil with γ=19 kN/m³, φ=35°. The Rankine active thrust is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 456, the minimum grade of concrete for reinforced concrete work is:
questions.solution: IS 456 specifies M20 as the minimum grade for RCC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The maximum permissible noise level in residential areas during daytime as per CPCB is:
questions.solution: Residential day: 55 dB(A); night: 45 dB(A). Commercial: 65/55. Industrial: 75/70.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Air-entrained concrete has improved:
questions.solution: Air entrainment creates microscopic air bubbles for freeze-thaw resistance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has G = 2.6, e = 0.8, S = 1.0. The water content is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The cross-sectional area of one 20 mm diameter bar is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) indicates:
questions.solution: BOD measures the oxygen needed to biodegrade organic matter, indicating organic pollution.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The compressive strength of concrete is measured after:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shear strength of a cohesionless soil is governed by:
questions.solution: For sands, shear strength = σ' tanφ (no cohesion).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an RC beam, if the depth of neutral axis is less than the limiting value, the beam is:
questions.solution: x_u < x_u,max → under-reinforced (steel yields first - ductile failure).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A combined sewer carries:
questions.solution: Combined sewer = domestic + industrial + stormwater (older systems).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The compressive strength of a brick used for load-bearing walls should not be less than:
questions.solution: Class 35 brick: min 3.5 N/mm² (IS 1077 for common burnt clay bricks). Actually lowest class is 3.5.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pile foundations are used when:
options: Soil at shallow depth has low bearing capacity
questions.solution: Piles transfer load to deeper bearing strata when surface soils are weak.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a theodolite, the line of sight should be perpendicular to:
questions.solution: Line of sight ⊥ horizontal axis (trunnion axis) for horizontal angle measurement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 80 MPa, σy = 60 MPa and τxy = 30 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 80 MPa, σy = 60 MPa, τxy = 30 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=70$, radius $=31.62$; $\sigma_1=70+31.62$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=101.62$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The error due to eccentricity of the vernier in a theodolite is eliminated by:
questions.solution: Vernier eccentricity error eliminated by averaging readings of both verniers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 10 mm diameter steel wire is wound around a drum of 1 m diameter. The bending stress induced (E = 200 GPa) is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The width of carriageway for a single lane road is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A beam has overall depth 550 mm, clear cover 30 mm and 25 mm main bars. The effective depth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sum of distribution factors at a rigid joint equals:
questions.solution: Distribution factors at any joint always sum to unity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Water flows through a pipe of diameter 0.15 m at 1 m/s. The discharge is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a well-graded soil, the coefficient of curvature Cc lies between:
questions.solution: Well-graded soils have a high uniformity coefficient and Cc between 1 and 3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of length 2 m carries a 10 kN load at its free end (E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴). The tip deflection (WL³/3EI) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An end-loaded cantilever has tip deflection $W L^3/3EI$.
**Formula.** $\delta=\tfrac{W L^3}{3 E I}$
**Given.** W = 10 kN, L = 2 m, E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{10\times10^3\times2^3}{3\times200\times10^9\times16\times10^{-6}}$ m.
**Answer.** $\delta\approx 8.3$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Manning's roughness coefficient n for concrete pipes is about:
questions.solution: n = 0.013 for concrete; 0.015 for brick; 0.025 for earth channels.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The gauge of a railway track in India for BG is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The refraction correction in levelling is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A beam has overall depth 400 mm, clear cover 40 mm and 16 mm main bars. The effective depth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A statically determinate truss has 12 members and 7 joints. The number of unknown reaction components if supported by a pin and roller is:
questions.solution: Pin = 2 reactions, roller = 1 reaction → total 3 unknown reactions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The difference in pressure between two points A and B in a pipe carrying water is measured by a U-tube manometer. If the manometric fluid is mercury (SG = 13.6) and the reading is 100 mm, the pressure difference is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 10 kPa and friction angle φ = 25°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 80 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 10 kPa, φ = 25°, σ = 80 kPa.
**Working.** $=10+80\times\tan 25^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=47.3$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 3 m carries a point load of 20 kN at the free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: BM_max = PL = 20×3 = 60 kNm at fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The break-point chlorination curve has a peak where:
options: All NH₃ is oxidized and residual starts rising
questions.solution: At breakpoint: added Cl₂ completely oxidizes NH₃; further addition increases free residual.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The angle of internal friction for bituminous concrete in shear test is about:
questions.solution: Bituminous mix φ = 30-45° (depends on aggregate grading and binder).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: GPS requires a minimum of how many satellites for 3D positioning?
questions.solution: 3 for 2D, 4 for 3D (x, y, z, t). More = better accuracy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The critical section for shear in a beam is at:
options: A distance d from the face of the support
questions.solution: Critical shear section = d from support (IS 456 clause 40.1).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnetic declination at a place is the angle between:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 80 MPa and 20 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 80 MPa, σ₃ = 20 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{80-20}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=30$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The deflection of a truss joint is calculated using:
questions.solution: Unit load method (virtual work) for truss deflections: δ = Σ(PkL/AE).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-ended column (E = 200 GPa, I = 4×10⁻⁶ m⁴, length 3 m) has an Euler buckling load of about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A pin-ended column buckles at the Euler critical load with effective length equal to the actual length.
**Formula.** $P_{cr}=\tfrac{\pi^2 E I}{L^2}$
**Given.** E = 200 GPa, I = 4×10⁻⁶ m⁴, L = 3 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{\pi^2\times200\times10^9\times4\times10^{-6}}{3^2}$ N.
**Answer.** $P_{cr}\approx 877.3$ kN.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a central point load of 40 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 40 kN, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{40\times4}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=40$ kNm at mid-span.

questions.solution: Terzaghi assumes: Darcy's law valid, soil homogeneous, k constant, 1D flow, etc.

questions.solution: Terzaghi's bearing capacity theory is the standard for shallow foundations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a venturimeter, the converging cone angle is usually:
questions.solution: Converging cone = 20°, diverging cone = 6-7° to avoid separation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The allowable bearing pressure for a raft foundation is generally:
questions.solution: Raft spreads load over larger area → lower settlement → higher allowable bearing pressure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-ended column (E = 200 GPa, I = 8×10⁻⁶ m⁴, length 4 m) has an Euler buckling load of about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A pin-ended column buckles at the Euler critical load with effective length equal to the actual length.
**Formula.** $P_{cr}=\tfrac{\pi^2 E I}{L^2}$
**Given.** E = 200 GPa, I = 8×10⁻⁶ m⁴, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{\pi^2\times200\times10^9\times8\times10^{-6}}{4^2}$ N.
**Answer.** $P_{cr}\approx 987$ kN.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The BM diagram of a cantilever with point load at tip is:
questions.solution: Cantilever + point load: BM diagram = linear (max at fixed end).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The hydraulic radius of a full-flowing circular pipe of diameter D is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The secondary consolidation occurs:
options: After primary consolidation, under constant effective stress
questions.solution: Secondary consolidation = creep under constant σ' after excess pore pressure dissipated.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3 m clay layer (Cc = 0.4, e₀ = 1) under an initial effective stress of 80 kPa receives a 40 kPa increment. The primary consolidation settlement is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Primary consolidation of a normally consolidated clay follows the logarithmic compression law.
**Formula.** $S_c=\tfrac{C_c H}{1+e_0}\log_{10}\tfrac{\sigma_0+\Delta\sigma}{\sigma_0}$
**Given.** C_c = 0.4, H = 3 m, e₀ = 1, σ₀ = 80 kPa, Δσ = 40 kPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.4\times3000}{1+1}\log_{10}\tfrac{80+40}{80}$ mm.
**Answer.** $S_c\approx 105.7$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Darcy's law states that the discharge velocity v equals:
questions.solution: Darcy's law: v = ki, where k is permeability and i is hydraulic gradient.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If LL = 60% and PL = 25%, the plasticity index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 15 kPa and friction angle φ = 20°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 80 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 15 kPa, φ = 20°, σ = 80 kPa.
**Working.** $=15+80\times\tan 20^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=44.12$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has an unconfined compressive strength of 100 kPa. The cohesion (assuming φ = 0) is:
questions.solution: For φ = 0, q_u = 2c_u → c_u = q_u/2 = 50 kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 5 m clay layer (Cc = 0.2, e₀ = 0.8) under an initial effective stress of 100 kPa receives a 60 kPa increment. The primary consolidation settlement is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Primary consolidation of a normally consolidated clay follows the logarithmic compression law.
**Formula.** $S_c=\tfrac{C_c H}{1+e_0}\log_{10}\tfrac{\sigma_0+\Delta\sigma}{\sigma_0}$
**Given.** C_c = 0.2, H = 5 m, e₀ = 0.8, σ₀ = 100 kPa, Δσ = 60 kPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.2\times5000}{1+0.8}\log_{10}\tfrac{100+60}{100}$ mm.
**Answer.** $S_c\approx 113.4$ mm.

questions.stem: The endurance limit of steel is approximately:
questions.solution: Endurance limit ~ 0.5 S_ut for steels (up to 10⁶ cycles).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-ended column (E = 210 GPa, I = 8×10⁻⁶ m⁴, length 5 m) has an Euler buckling load of about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A pin-ended column buckles at the Euler critical load with effective length equal to the actual length.
**Formula.** $P_{cr}=\tfrac{\pi^2 E I}{L^2}$
**Given.** E = 210 GPa, I = 8×10⁻⁶ m⁴, L = 5 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{\pi^2\times210\times10^9\times8\times10^{-6}}{5^2}$ N.
**Answer.** $P_{cr}\approx 663.2$ kN.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 10 m carries a UDL of 10 kN/m. The maximum bending moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of length 4 m carries a 8 kN load at its free end (E = 200 GPa, I = 10×10⁻⁶ m⁴). The tip deflection (WL³/3EI) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An end-loaded cantilever has tip deflection $W L^3/3EI$.
**Formula.** $\delta=\tfrac{W L^3}{3 E I}$
**Given.** W = 8 kN, L = 4 m, E = 200 GPa, I = 10×10⁻⁶ m⁴.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{8\times10^3\times4^3}{3\times200\times10^9\times10\times10^{-6}}$ m.
**Answer.** $\delta\approx 85.3$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of length 3 m carries a point load of 15 kN at the tip. The maximum BM is:
questions.solution: BM_max = W×L = 15×3 = 45 kNm at fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 140 MPa and 40 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 140 MPa, σ₃ = 40 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{140-40}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=50$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever 3 m long carries a 5 kN load at its free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM at fixed end = W × L = 5 × 3 = 15 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries a point load of 20 kN at 2 m from the left support. The reaction at the left support is:
questions.solution: Taking moments about right: Rₐ × 6 = 20 × 4 → Rₐ = 80/6 = 13.33 kN.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using Manning's formula with n = 0.015, hydraulic radius R = 1 m and slope S = 0.0009, the mean velocity is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Manning's equation gives mean velocity from roughness, hydraulic radius and bed slope.
**Formula.** $V=\tfrac1n R^{2/3} S^{1/2}$
**Given.** n = 0.015, R = 1 m, S = 0.0009.
**Working.** $=\tfrac1{0.015}\times1^{2/3}\times\sqrt{0.0009}$.
**Answer.** $V\approx 2$ m/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The plasticity index of a soil is:
questions.solution: Plasticity Index = Liquid Limit − Plastic Limit.

questions.solution: M40 = characteristic compressive strength of 40 MPa at 28 days.
